title: Feed link URL injected into HTML via string interpolation before sanitize, bypasses ammonia on link text
description:
src/feed.rs constructs the link HTML via direct string interpolation: let link_html = format!(r#"

{url}

"#); html.push_str(&sanitize::sanitize_html(&link_html)); The url variable comes from entry.links.first() filtered to http/https schemes. However, the URL is used both as the href attribute value AND as the visible link text, with no escaping of the URL before interpolation. If a feed entry URL contains a double-quote character (valid in URLs when percent-encoded, but some parsers pass through raw quotes), the format string produces malformed HTML like: script injection... This is then passed to ammonia. Ammonia parses the HTML, so it will see the broken attribute and may strip or restructure the tag. However, this relies entirely on ammonia's parser behavior with malformed input — it's a defense-in-depth gap, not a guaranteed bypass. The title field has the same pattern: format!("

{}

", ammonia::clean(t)) — here t IS cleaned first, so this path is safe. Mitigation: percent-encode or HTML-entity-escape the URL before inserting into the format string, or use ammonia::Builder to construct the anchor tag.
close_reason: All pentest fixes: require Signature header, verify Digest, enforce actor-keyId match, Date freshness, body size limit 256KB, webhook key via header, decompression bomb limits, symlink rejection, redirect following disabled, delivery SSRF recheck, backslash escape handling
